mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and deteriorating corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer spending and construction activity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns as corporate revenue expectations rise.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, with a hit rate below 45 percent over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to immediate positive S&P 500 returns over the following week during economic expansions.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during expansion phases, with a statistically insignificant slope coefficient in OLS regression.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product confirms strengthening economic activity, which supports higher corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4 percent monthly threshold forces expectations of tighter monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 60 percent of months where CPIAUCSL rises above the 0.4 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that boosts consumer income and corporate earnings, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal generates a negative or flat return distribution over 20-day horizons in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden drop in industrial production below its 6-month moving average signals a supply-side contraction that reduces corporate output and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of instances where INDPRO crosses below its 6-month moving average.
